page.title: Blueprint Modular � Python building blocks for your UIs
page.description: Import from bpm, the rest is done. Components, docs, deployment. No front-end.

hero.title:
Building blocks ready.
You write the logic.
hero.desc: Import from bpm, assemble metrics, tables, forms. No HTML, no JS. One codebase.

pitch.heading: Why Blueprint Modular
pitch.item1: Reusable components � Text, data, charts, forms, layout. All in the docs.
pitch.item2: Python only � No front-end to wire. You declare, the UI updates.
pitch.item3: Docs and deployment included � Installation, API, VPS/Docker deployment step by step.

diff.heading: What BPM offers that others don't
diff.intro: Granular reactivity, design system, registry and config-driven layout: four strengths no other Python data framework offers natively.
diff.item1: Granular reactivity + simple syntax � Targeted component updates (refs, reactive store) without full script re-run.
diff.item2: Consistent design system (bpm.*) � Centralised tokens; customisation via CSS variables.
diff.item3: Registry $ and @ � Reactive refs for state; decorators for pages, sidebar, cache.
diff.item4: Config-driven layout � One app.config.js for everything; duplicate an app = edit that file.

cta.text: Everything is documented: installation, components, API reference, deployment.
